classObjective: Use angle relationships formed by a transversal to prove that two lines are parallel.
workedExamples: 12-9, Example 1 — Determine Which Lines Are Parallel Given Angle Relationships
12-9, Example 2 — Find the Value of x That Makes Lines Parallel
warmUp: What must be true about corresponding angles if two lines are parallel?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: corresponding angles converse; alternate interior/exterior converses; consecutive interior converse; proving parallelism
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice using angle relationships to prove lines are parallel
independentPractice: Practice problems using angle relationships to establish parallel lines
assessment: Exit ticket: prove that two lines are parallel given that alternate interior angles are congruent
capReflection: How do the converse theorems for parallel lines differ from the basic parallel line theorems?
